Remote mode has no local git/gh CLI." 11→--- 12→ 13→# RemoteGitHub Gateway 14→ 15→REST API-based GitHub operations without a local git or `gh` CLI dependency. session.assistant: **Step 11**: Update `rebase-confirmation-workflow.md` — expand the in-progress path to explain why tracking check is bypassed.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Rebase Confirmation Workflow 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying the erk pr rebase command" 5→ - "adding conflict confirmation UI to CLI commands" 6→ - "understanding rebase convergence paths" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "launching Claude for conflict resolution without showing conflicted files first" 9→ warning: "Always show conflicted files and get user confirmation before launching Claude. See rebase-confirmation-workflow.md." 10→--- 11→ 12→# Rebase Confirmation Workflow 13→ 14→The `erk pr rebase` command shows conflicted files and gets user confirmation before launching Claude for conflict resolution. This prevents users from being dropped into a Claude session without knowing what needs resolving. 15→ 16→## Three Convergence Paths 17→ 18→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rebase_cmd.py --> 19→ 20→All three entry paths converge at the same confirmation point: 21→ 22→### 1. Graphite Restack Path 23→ 24→When Graphite is enabled, uses `gt restack --no-interactive`. If restack succeeds (no conflicts), returns immediately. If conflicts remain, falls through to confirmation. 25→ 26→### 2. Git Rebase Path 27→ 28→When Graphite is not enabled and no rebase is in progress, requires `--target` flag. Runs `git rebase` onto target. If rebase succeeds, returns. If conflicts remain, falls through to confirmation. 29→ 30→### 3. In-Progress Rebase Path
... [34 lines omitted] ...

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
### 3. In-Progress Rebase Path
Detects an existing rebase in progress via `is_rebase_in_progress()`. Displays "Rebase in progress" message and falls through to confirmation.

### 3. In-Progress Rebase Path
Detects an existing rebase in progress via `is_rebase_in_p...[truncated 262 chars]...ked()` assertion. Displays "Rebase in progress" message and falls through to confirmation.

session.tool_result: docs/learned/testing/index.md:37:- **[remote-paths-testing.md](remote-paths-testing.md)** — testing commands with --repo flag, writing tests for remote-mode CLI commands, using NoRepoSentinel or FakeRemoteGitHub in tests docs/learned/cli/index.md:67:- **[ref-resolution-patterns.md](ref-resolution-patterns.md)** — adding --ref or --ref-current to a CLI command, working with dispatch ref resolution, understanding how workflow dispatch ref is determined docs/learned/cli/index.md:68:- **[repo-resolution-pattern.md](repo-resolution-pattern.md)** — adding --repo flag to a CLI command, working with resolve_owner_repo, creating a command that operates on remote repositories, testing commands with NoRepoSentinel docs/learned/planning/index.md:14:- **[consolidate-learn-plans-workflow.md](consolidate-learn-plans-workflow.md)** — working with consolidate-learn-plans workflow, modifying the learn plan consolidation pipeline, adding new dispatch-based workflows via RemoteGitHub docs/learned/architecture/index.md:130:- **[sync-branch-to-sha-pattern.md](sync-branch-to-sha-pattern.md)** — syncing a local branch to a remote SHA, updating a branch that might be checked out in a worktree, working with dispatch_helpers.py docs/learned/architecture/index.md:135:- **[unified-dispatch-pattern.md](unified-dispatch-pattern.md)** — adding a new workflow to erk launch, modifying launch_cmd.py dispatch logic, understanding how local and remote dispatch are unified docs/learned/ci/index.md:38:- **[merge-gate-jobs.md](merge-gate-jobs.md)** — adding a CI job that blocks merging bad content, working with no-impl-context CI job, understanding CI job skip conditions for draft PRs
session.assistant: All 7 new docs are indexed. Now let me verify cross-references are valid:
session.tool_result: docs/learned/planning/incremental-dispatch.md:46:Uses `sync_branch_to_sha()` from `dispatch_helpers.py` for branch synchronization — safely handles both checked-out and non-checked-out branches. When a branch is checked out in a worktree, `sync_branch_to_sha` uses `git reset --hard` for atomic ref + index + working tree sync. See [sync_branch_to_sha Pattern](../architecture/sync-branch-to-sha-pattern.md). docs/learned/planning/incremental-dispatch.md:52:- [sync_branch_to_sha Pattern](../architecture/sync-branch-to-sha-pattern.md) — safe branch sync for checked-out branches docs/learned/architecture/tripwires.md:141:**calling update_local_ref on a branch without checking if it's checked out** → Read [sync_branch_to_sha Pattern](sync-branch-to-sha-pattern.md) first. Use sync_branch_to_sha() instead — it handles checked-out branches by using git reset --hard. Raw update_local_ref on checked-out branches causes index desync. docs/learned/architecture/tripwires.md:363:**using git checkout to sync a branch to a remote SHA** → Read [sync_branch_to_sha Pattern](sync-branch-to-sha-pattern.md) first. Use sync_branch_to_sha() from dispatch_helpers.py. It avoids checkout and handles both checked-out and non-checked-out branches. docs/learned/architecture/git-plumbing-patterns.md:95:**For general-purpose branch sync**, use `sync_branch_to_sha()` from `dispatch_helpers.py`. It handles the checked-out case by using `git reset --hard` for atomic ref + index + working tree sync, and refuses if the worktree has uncommitted changes. See [sync_branch_to_sha Pattern](sync-branch-to-sha-pattern.md). docs/learned/architecture/git-plumbing-patterns.md:147:- [sync_branch_to_sha Pattern](sync-branch-to-sha-pattern.md) - Safe branch sync for checked-out branches docs/learned/architecture/index.md:130:- **[sync-branch-to-sha-pattern.md](sync-branch-to-sha-pattern.md)** — syncing a local branch to a remote SHA, updating a branch that might be checked out in a worktree, working with dispatch_helpers.py

session.tool_result: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable
session.assistant:
Plan execution complete. Here's the summary:
## Summary
Implemented all 11 steps from consolidated plan #9059, documenting gaps from 23 erk-learn plans.
### New Documents Created (7)
1. **`docs/learned/cli/repo-resolution-pattern.md`** — `resolve_owner_repo`, `@repo_option`, `@resolved_repo_option`, `get_remote_github()` 2. **`docs/learned/architecture/unified-dispatch-pattern.md`** — single RemoteGitHub-based handler pattern for all `erk launch` workflows 3. **`docs/learned/architecture/sync-branch-to-sha-pattern.md`** — safe branch sync handling checked-out worktrees via `git reset --hard` 4. **`docs/learned/ci/merge-gate-jobs.md`** — `no-impl-context` CI job pattern for merge blocking 5. **`docs/learned/cli/ref-resolution-patterns.md`** — `--ref`/`--ref-current` resolution chain and remote guard 6. **`docs/learned/testing/remote-paths-testing.md`** — `NoRepoSentinel` + `FakeRemoteGitHub` test patterns 7. **`docs/learned/planning/consolidate-learn-plans-workflow.md`** — consolidation workflow, dispatch module, CI pipeline
### Existing Documents Updated (4)
8. **`git-plumbing-patterns.md`** — added `sync_branch_to_sha` reference and cross-link 9. **`incremental-dispatch.md`** — updated to reference `sync_branch_to_sha` for branch sync 10. **`remote-github-gateway.md`** — added `get_pr()` method, `RemotePRInfo`/`RemotePRNotFound` types, `resolved_repo_option` 11. **`rebase-confirmation-workflow.md`** — expanded in-progress path to explain tracking check bypass
### CI & PR
- All tests pass (5953 unit tests) - All lint/type checks pass - `erk docs sync` regenerated indexes with 872 tripwires - PR #9059 updated and submitted: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9059
